objective: Add CI enforcement for the STANDARDS.md §PDCAR functional acceptance auditor gate: reusable check-acceptance-audit.yml workflow, Python check script, caller workflow, pytest tests, and governance artifacts.
scope_boundary: Create tests/test_check_acceptance_audit.py — pytest coverage for all exemption paths.
Create governance artifacts: execution scope, cross-review, acceptance audit, closeout, structured plan, handoff.
out_of_scope: Editing STANDARDS.md, existing hooks, or existing workflows beyond adding the new check to ci.yml.
Changing any agent definitions or agent registry.
Any schema changes to existing governance schemas.
Closing parent epics or changing non-slice governance policy.
research_summary: STANDARDS.md §PDCAR requires a functional-acceptance-auditor PASS artifact before slice merge. Issue #653 Slice H added the auditor agent and schema. Issue #659 wires the CI enforcement gate so PRs without a matching PASS artifact in raw/acceptance-audits/ are blocked.

sprints.name: CI acceptance audit gate
sprints.goal: Wire the functional-acceptance-auditor PASS check into GitHub Actions CI so every governed implementation slice PR must have a matching PASS artifact before merge.
tasks: Create .github/scripts/check_acceptance_audit.py with branch issue extraction, audit-dir scan, PASS match, and exemption paths.
Create .github/workflows/check-acceptance-audit.yml as a reusable workflow_call workflow.
Create tests/test_check_acceptance_audit.py with pytest coverage for all code paths.
Create governance artifacts: execution scope, cross-review, acceptance audit, closeout, handoff.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: check-acceptance-audit.yml exists and is a valid reusable workflow.
AC2: On a PR with no acceptance audit artifact: check fails with ::error:: and exit 1.
AC3: On a PR with a PASS artifact matching the issue number: check passes.
AC4: On a non-issue branch: check passes (exempt).
AC5: On a planning-only PR: check passes (exempt).
AC8: Functional acceptance auditor PASS artifact present.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm the CI gate implementation is bounded to the write list, that acceptance criteria AC1-AC8 are fully represented, and that no existing workflows are mutated.
specialist_reviews.summary: OpenAI-family architecture review accepted the CI gate package as bounded, additive, and correctly mapped to the PDCAR acceptance audit enforcement requirement.

alternate_model_review.summary: OpenAI-family cross-review accepted the implementation-ready package. Anthropic-family worker executes bounded delivery.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement CI acceptance audit gate: check script, reusable workflow, caller, tests, and governance artifacts under the named write boundary only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Create all bounded files, run pytest, file validation and closeout artifacts.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit outside the user-provided allowed write paths is a material deviation.
Any change to STANDARDS.md, hooks, existing workflows, scripts, packages, or wiki paths is a material deviation.
Any self-audit artifact that does not validate against the acceptance audit schema is a material deviation.
Any closeout that omits the required validation or cross-review references is a material deviation.
